chapter_title.en: Chapter 63 – The Divorce of disavowment by payment (Al-Mubarat)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/240)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/240)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) هَلْ يَكُونُ خُلْعٌ أَوْ مُبَارَاةٌ إِلَّا بِطُهْرٍ فَقَالَ لَا يَكُونُ إِلَّا بِطُهْرٍ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazaan, and abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, altogether from Safwan, from Abdul Rahman Bin Al Hajjaj who said,
‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws, ‘Does either Al-Khul’a or Al-Mubaraat happen except by purity?’ So heasws said: ‘It does not happen except by purity (from menstruation)’.
en.sarwar: 8. Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan and Abu Ali al-Ash‘ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al- Jabbar all from Safwan from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajj aj who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if al-Mubarat (disavowing) or al-Khul‘ can take place without Hayd (menses)-free period. He (the Imam) said, ‘It cannot take place without Hayd (menses)- free period.’”
